sync.deviceNotice: These settings are shared across every device signed in to this server, except items marked 'This device only'.

global.themeSystemUnsupported: Samsung Internet does not support system theme detection. Please select Dark or Light manually.
global.displayNamePlaceholder: How should the agent address you?
global.displayNameDesc: The agent will use this name to address you. Leave empty for no specific form of address.

permissionDesc.latest: New sessions keep the permission mode from the previous session
permissionDesc.plan: Suggests a plan before code changes
permissionDesc.default: Always asks for confirmation before editing files

sdk.summary: Hammoc's default engine — most stable, broadest feature support

points.label: Response
points.value: Real-time, token-by-token streaming; answers flow in immediately

points.label: Features
points.value: Every model and Hammoc feature, no restrictions

points.label: Environment
points.value: Direct call to Anthropic's Agent SDK (the official path)

points.label: Maturity
points.value: Generally available and stable, even in long sessions

cli.summary: Runs your terminal's interactive Claude Code as-is — same subscription environment

points.label: Response
points.value: Block-by-block updates (paragraphs, tool runs), not token by token

points.label: Features
points.value: Core chat focus; some features still limited

points.label: Environment
points.value: Runs in your subscription (Max) environment; unaffected by billing-policy changes

points.label: Maturity
points.value: Beta; first response may take a brief moment to start

global.autoApproveSafetyChecks: Auto-approve safety checks in Bypass mode
global.autoApproveSafetyChecksDesc: When enabled, CLI safety check prompts are automatically approved without user confirmation in Bypass mode.

permissionSyncDesc.always: Always syncs and remembers per-session permission mode

global.markdownDesc: Default mode when opening markdown files.
global.fileExplorerDesc: Default view mode when opening the file explorer.
global.panelDefaultOpenDesc: Automatically open the quick panel when entering a session on desktop.
global.panelDefaultSideDesc: Default side for the quick panel. 'Last used' keeps the side from the previous session.
global.chatTimeoutDesc: Time to automatically abort request when no response is received.
global.cliSettingsDesc: Applied when the CLI conversation engine is active.
global.cliShowThinkingSummariesDesc: Surface Claude's thinking summaries while the CLI engine generates a response. (Requested per session; whether summaries actually appear can depend on your Claude version/auth.)
global.cliResumeChoiceDesc: When resuming a large/old session, Claude asks whether to resume from a summary or the full session. 'Ask each time' shows it as a card so you choose; the auto options pick for you so the turn never blocks. CLI mode only.
global.cliShowGenerationProgressDesc: Display a live '↓ N tokens · Ns' indicator while a response is being generated.
global.cliMirrorThrottleMsDesc: How often the mirror refreshes to claude's current screen. Lower = smoother / more bandwidth, higher = calmer (default 200ms).
global.cliScreenStallMsDesc: If claude's screen shows no change at all for this long during a reply, surface a "looks stuck — Stop?" prompt (advisory — never auto-aborts; you decide). 0 disables; default 20000ms. Only active while the mirror is on.
global.cliBinaryPathDesc: Manual override for the claude executable path. Leave empty to auto-detect.
global.cliPtyMirrorDesc: Show a read-only terminal mirroring claude's screen during CLI-mode chats (default on — turn it off here). New or refreshed browsers sync to the current screen, so every device shows the same view.

toast.serverTimeout: Server response timed out. Please check manually.
toast.settingSavedFailed: Failed to save settings. Please try again.

advanced.serverRestartDesc: Builds the entire project and restarts the server in production mode
advanced.systemPromptWarningDetail: Incorrect system prompt modifications can significantly degrade response quality. Keep the default if you're unsure.
advanced.promptNoProject: Select a project to view the resolved system prompt.
advanced.migrationBanner: Your system prompt has been restructured. System-maintained sections are now managed automatically above. Please review your custom area below and remove any content that duplicates the fixed sections.
advanced.maxThinkingTokensDesc: Maximum tokens for model reasoning process (1,024 ~ 128,000)
advanced.defaultEffortDesc: Default thinking effort level for new sessions. When not set, the SDK default (High) is used. XHigh and Max only apply on supported models; on unsupported models they fall back to High.
advanced.chatCheckpointingDesc: Save file snapshots during chat for rewind/restore
advanced.queueCheckpointingDesc: Save file snapshots during queue execution (increases JSONL file size)
advanced.showThinkingBlocksDesc: Displays collapsed 'thinking' blocks above responses on recent models (Opus 4.7+). Turn off for slightly faster first-token latency (cost is unchanged).
advanced.cardEntranceAnimationDesc: Streaming message and tool cards fade and slide up one by one as they appear, instead of popping in all at once. Applies to both engines (cosmetic; on by default).
advanced.autoCompactEnabledDesc: When the context window fills up, automatically compact the conversation (summarize older turns) so the session keeps going instead of stalling. Applies to both engines; on by default. Turn off to keep the full history and compact manually with /compact.
advanced.groupCommonDesc: Settings that apply to both the SDK and CLI engines.
advanced.groupSdkDesc: Settings used only when the conversation engine is set to SDK.
advanced.groupCliDesc: Settings used only when the conversation engine is set to CLI.

confirm.serverRestart: This will rebuild and restart the server. Continue?
confirm.updateVersion: This will update to v{{version}} and restart the server. Continue?
confirm.resetAllSettings:
Reset all project settings.

• Model/Permission Mode overrides will be removed
• Project hiding will be disabled

Continue?

telegram.loadError: An error occurred while loading Telegram settings.
telegram.setupStep1Suffix: to create a bot and get the Bot Token
telegram.envPriority: Preferences setting takes priority over environment variable.
telegram.baseUrlHint: URL included in notifications for quick access. Use your internal IP instead of localhost.
telegram.enableHint: Please set Bot Token and Chat ID first.
telegram.notifyPermission: Permission request — When 🔐 permission confirmation or ❓ question is needed
telegram.notifyError: Error — When ❌ an error occurs during streaming
telegram.notifyQueueStart: Queue start — When queue execution starts
telegram.notifyQueueComplete: Queue complete — After all items are processed
telegram.notifyQueueError: Queue error — On @pauseword match or SDK error
telegram.notifyQueueInput: Queue input request — When user response is needed
telegram.alwaysNotify: Always notify — Send even when session is visible
telegram.alwaysNotifyHint: By default, notifications are suppressed when you're viewing the session.

project.loadError: An error occurred while loading settings.

basicChatItems: Type a message in the chat input and press Enter (desktop) or the send button (mobile) to send
Image attachment: Upload images via the clip icon or paste (Ctrl+V)
New session: Start a new chat session with the "+" button in the header
Switch sessions: Access previous sessions via the clock icon
slashCommandItems: Type / in the chat input to see available commands
Frequently used commands can be added to ★ favorites
Add favorites: Click ★ or long-press in the command palette
Favorites appear as quick access buttons above the input area
help.permissionPlanDesc: Suggests a plan before code changes
help.permissionDefaultDesc: Always asks for confirmation before editing files
help.permissionNote: You can change the default Permission Mode in Settings > Global Settings or Project Settings.
bmadMethodItems: BMad Method is an AI-powered development workflow framework
Install BMad Core to a project via the "BMad Setup" button on the project page
After installation, quickly invoke specialized agents from the BMad agent button (bottom-right of chat)
Agents: SM (Scrum Master), PM (Project Manager), Dev (Developer), QA (Quality Assurance), etc.

info.title: Claude Code Account
info.refreshFailed: Could not fetch account info. Try starting a chat first, then retry.
info.emptyHint: Account info fills in automatically after you send your first chat, or click Refresh to fetch now.
info.usageEmpty: Rate limit info not available. Available for Claude.ai subscribers.

account.changeSuccess: Password changed. Please sign in again.

bmad.title: BMad core-config.yaml
bmad.requiredKeyEmptyWarning: This key is required by BMad — saving an empty value may prevent agents from loading correctly.

markdownExploder.label: Markdown Exploder
markdownExploder.description: Use the md-tree command to explode documents into shards.

qaLocation.label: QA Location
qaLocation.description: Directory where QA gates and assessments are written.

prdVersion.label: PRD Version
prdVersion.description: PRD convention version (e.g. v3 or v4).

prdSharded.label: PRD Sharded
prdSharded.description: Whether epics live in separate sharded files.

prdShardedLocation.label: PRD Sharded Location
prdShardedLocation.description: Directory holding the sharded PRD/epic files.

epicFilePattern.label: Epic File Pattern
epicFilePattern.description: Glob for epic filenames; {n} matches the epic number.

architectureVersion.label: Architecture Version
architectureVersion.description: Architecture convention version (e.g. v3 or v4).

architectureSharded.label: Architecture Sharded
architectureSharded.description: Whether the architecture is split into component files.

architectureShardedLocation.label: Architecture Sharded Location
architectureShardedLocation.description: Directory holding the sharded architecture files.

customTechnicalDocuments.label: Custom Technical Documents
customTechnicalDocuments.description: Extra technical documents agents should load (optional).

devLoadAlwaysFiles.label: Dev Load Always Files
devLoadAlwaysFiles.description: Files the dev agent loads for every task.

updateOnCreate.label: Update On Create
updateOnCreate.description: Files updated when a brownfield epic is created.

doNotUpdate.label: Do Not Update
doNotUpdate.description: Path patterns excluded from brownfield epic updates.

devDebugLog.label: Dev Debug Log
devDebugLog.description: File where the dev agent logs repeated failures.

devStoryLocation.label: Dev Story Location
devStoryLocation.description: Directory where story files are created and read.

slashPrefix.label: Slash Prefix
slashPrefix.description: Prefix for BMad slash commands (e.g. BMad → /BMad:agents:dev).

array.patternHint: Glob/path pattern (e.g. a directory or a glob).

unknownKeys.title: Unknown Keys
unknownKeys.description: Keys this editor does not recognize (BMad schema extensions or custom keys). They are preserved as-is on save.
unknownKeys.readOnly: Read-only — edit these in Raw mode.

raw.parseError: YAML syntax error — fix it in Raw mode and try again.
raw.unsavedFormChangesWarning: You have unsaved form changes — switching to Raw will discard them.
raw.unsavedRawChangesWarning: You have unsaved raw changes — switching to Form will discard them.

externalChange.banner: This file was changed outside Hammoc.

stale.body: The file was changed outside Hammoc. Reload to get the latest, or overwrite with your version.

empty.title: No skills installed.
empty.description: Add skills under <projectRoot>/.claude/skills/ or ~/.claude/skills/, or install a plugin bundle.

bundle.title: Bundle resources
bundle.binaryReadOnly: Binary file — Hammoc UI cannot edit it.
bundle.truncatedAtDepth: Bundle tree truncated at the depth limit.

conflict.title: Skill name conflict
conflict.renameInvalid: Invalid name — empty, unchanged, or contains reserved characters.

error.crossDevice: This skill lives across a mount boundary — copy with an external file manager.

editor.rawParseError: Frontmatter cannot be parsed — fix the fenced YAML to return to Form mode.
editor.staleBanner: This skill was changed externally — your save was rejected. Latest contents loaded.

malformed.title: Some skills were skipped because their frontmatter is invalid:

empty.title: No MCP servers configured.
empty.description: Add an mcpServers entry to <projectRoot>/.mcp.json or your global ~/.claude/.mcp.json, or install a plugin bundle that includes MCP servers.
empty.noGlobalSupport: Claude Code does not recognise a global MCP file on this version — only project-scope and plugin-bundle MCPs are visible.

banner.freshSpawn: Takes effect on your next user message.

secret.title: Secret-looking values detected
secret.intro: The following fields look like secrets and will be copied as plain text:
secret.ack: I understand and want to copy with secrets.

conflict.title: Server name conflict
conflict.renameInvalid: Invalid name — empty, unchanged, or contains reserved characters.
conflict.pluginRootWarning: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} reference will not resolve outside the plugin bundle.

error.crossDevice: This server lives across a mount boundary — copy with an external file manager.

typeChange.title: Type change will discard fields
typeChange.intro: Switching from {{from}} to {{to}} will remove fields that don't apply to the new type.

editor.staleBanner: This server config was changed externally. Reload from disk or overwrite with your edits.

malformed.title: Some MCP servers were skipped because their config is invalid:

contextBuilder.title: Context Builder
contextBuilder.subtitle: Inject reference files and dynamic variables into every new session via a SessionStart hook.
contextBuilder.registeredNote: A SessionStart hook is registered in .claude/settings.json and regenerated on every change.

stale.body: The manifest was modified outside Hammoc. Reload the latest, or overwrite with your version?

size.warn: Assembled context is {{total}}, nearing the {{cap}}-character SessionStart hook output cap. Beyond it the content spills to a file + preview instead of being injected directly.
size.over: Assembled context is {{total}}, over the {{cap}}-character SessionStart hook output cap — it will spill to a file + preview instead of being injected directly. Trim the reference files.

files.title: Reference files
files.help: Files are read fresh at every session start and injected as context.
files.missing: File not found on disk — it will inject a placeholder until restored.
files.tokenApproxNote: Approximate (precise tokens after the observability feature ships).

variables.title: Dynamic variables
variables.help: Recomputed at every session start with their latest values.

activeBmadStory.label: Active BMad story
activeBmadStory.description: Title + status of the most recently modified story file.

recentCommits.label: Recent commits
recentCommits.description: The N most recent commit subjects.

uncommittedCount.label: Uncommitted files
uncommittedCount.description: Count of files with uncommitted changes.

commands.title: Custom commands
commands.warning: Custom commands run automatically at every session start. Only add commands you trust.
commands.acknowledge: I understand this command runs automatically at every session start.
commands.secretWarning: This command may contain a secret.
commands.secretNotice: {{count}} custom command(s) may contain a secret — review before sharing this project.

observability.title: Observability
observability.subtitle: Track MCP tool calls and how much of the context window each harness element consumes.

mcp.title: MCP calls
mcp.empty: No MCP calls recorded yet. Run a session that uses tools to populate this.

tokens.title: Token attribution
tokens.help: Estimated token weight of each harness element. Inline values are byte-based approximations; click “Exact count” for the official number.
tokens.approxNote: ~ values are byte-based estimates (size ÷ 4); multibyte scripts may differ. Use “Exact count” for the official token count.

tokenizer.title: Approximation tokenizer
tokenizer.help: Tokenizer for the server-side approximation. The inline ~ hint is always byte size ÷ 4.
tokenizer.notAdopted: Only the byte size ÷ 4 heuristic is available — the tokenizer-grade tier was not adopted (spike #1: it drifts 25–45% from the official count on Claude 4.x). The exact count path is always available.

managedBadge.contextBuilderTooltip: Managed by the Hammoc context builder — edit it from the Context Builder panel.
managedBadge.editWarning: This entry is managed by the Hammoc context builder — editing it directly will break sync. Edit it from the Context Builder panel instead.

empty.title: No hooks configured.
empty.description: Add a hook entry to <projectRoot>/.claude/settings.json or your global ~/.claude/settings.json under the hooks field, or install a plugin bundle that registers hooks.
empty.perEvent: No hooks for {{event}}. Click + Add to create one.

banner.freshSpawn: Takes effect on your next user message.
banner.promptCost: Prompt-type hooks invoke the LLM each time and incur cost/latency.

hook.parallelExecutionBadge_one: Runs in parallel with 1 hook from another source.
hook.parallelExecutionBadge_other: Runs in parallel with {{count}} hooks from other sources.

warning.title: Review hook before copying
warning.commandIntro: This hook runs an arbitrary shell command on every trigger. Make sure you trust the source.
warning.promptIntro: This hook invokes the LLM on every trigger and incurs cost and latency.
warning.ack: I have reviewed the body and want to copy.

conflict.title: An identical hook already exists
conflict.intro: An entry with the same matcher and body already exists in {{targetScope}}.
conflict.duplicateInfo: Duplicate added — both entries will run in parallel.
conflict.pluginRootWarning: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} reference will not resolve outside the plugin bundle.

matcher.invalidRegex: Matcher must be a valid regex.
matcher.sharedWithSiblings: This matcher is shared with {{count}} other hook(s) in the same group.

applyOption.title: Apply matcher change to:
applyOption.sharedWithSiblings: All {{count}} hook(s) in this group (default — keeps the group intact)
applyOption.splitFromGroup: Only this hook (extract it into its own group)
applyOption.afterSplitToast: This hook moved to a new group — sibling hooks kept the original matcher.

editor.matcherHelp: Matcher info — regex/pipes/wildcards allowed. Empty = match all.
editor.timeoutHint: 0 or empty = use Claude Code default.
editor.promptTypeUnsupported: This CLI version does not support prompt-type hooks.

required.command: command body is required for type=command.
required.prompt: prompt body is required for type=prompt.

tokenGuide.positionalArgs: $1, $2 — first/second positional argument from the slash command
tokenGuide.argumentsAll: $ARGUMENTS — full argument string passed to the command
tokenGuide.bashExec: !`command` — executes a shell command and inserts the output

warnings.argumentsWithoutHint: This command uses $1 / $ARGUMENTS but argument-hint is unset — palette users will not see argument guidance.
warnings.bashWithoutAllowedTool: This command uses !`...` shell execution but allowed-tools does not include Bash — the SDK will block execution.
warnings.pluginRootInNonPlugin: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} only resolves in plugin bundles — in project/user commands it expands to an empty string.

create.nameHelp: Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens. 3–50 characters. Cannot start or end with a hyphen.
create.descriptionHelp: Describe when this agent should be auto-selected. Including <example> blocks improves auto-selection quality.
create.toolsHelp: Use the editor after creation to switch to a custom allow-list.

errors.namePattern: Use 3–50 lowercase letters, digits, hyphens. Cannot start or end with hyphen.
errors.nameUnchanged: New name must differ from the source.

name.invalidPattern: Use 3–50 lowercase letters, digits, hyphens. Cannot start or end with hyphen.

warnings.noExampleBlock: This agent has no <example> blocks — auto-selection quality may suffer. Use the + Add example button to insert a template.

reason.nameMismatch: {{name}} — frontmatter.name does not match the file stem

plugin.emptyHint: Install plugins with `/plugin install` in the Claude Code CLI, then return here.

banner.freshSpawn: Takes effect on your next user message.
banner.longStream: Takes effect after the current chat ends. Start a new session to apply immediately.

claudeMd.panelHelp: Both files are loaded into every Claude Code session — global first, project last (project takes precedence). Subdirectory CLAUDE.md files are loaded on demand when Claude reads files there; this panel only manages the two top-level files.

empty.title: No CLAUDE.md exists yet at this location.
empty.userHint: Global CLAUDE.md is included in every Claude Code session across all projects.
empty.projectHint: Project CLAUDE.md is included in every Claude Code session for this project.

create.confirmBody: An empty CLAUDE.md will be created at this location. You can fill it in afterwards.

staleBanner.message: This file changed externally. Reload from disk (your edits will be lost) or overwrite to keep your edits?

mode.appendHint: Pick the H2 sections to append to the end of the target file.
mode.overwriteHint: Replace the target file entirely with the source content.

copy.noH2Banner: The source file has no ## H2 headings, so section append is unavailable. Use overwrite or add headings first.
copy.overwriteWarning: All current content of the target file will be replaced. Continue?

errors.noSelection: Pick at least one section to append.

error.fileExists: CLAUDE.md already exists at this location.

systemBadge.hammoc.help: Hammoc-native asset. Stored under <projectRoot>/.hammoc/snippets/, ~/.hammoc/snippets/, or the server bundle, and substituted as %name% in chat input. Claude Code CLI does NOT recognize these files.
systemBadge.claudeCode.help: Stores starring + ordering metadata for Claude Code /slash commands at ~/.hammoc/preferences.json. Edit the underlying command bodies under Slash commands.

errors.namePattern: Use letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens only.
errors.nameTaken: A snippet with this name already exists.

conflict.title: Snippet already exists at target
conflict.description: A snippet named "{{name}}" already exists in {{scope}}.

overwrite.label: Overwrite
overwrite.help: Replace the target body with the source body.

rename.label: Copy with new name
rename.help: Save the source body under a different name.

renameError.unchanged: Pick a name distinct from the original.
renameError.pattern: Use letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens only.

editor.title: Edit snippet
editor.bodyHint: Use %name% to reference other snippets, {arg1}/{arg2}/… for positional arguments, and {context} for the trailing context block.
editor.bundledReadOnly: This is a bundled snippet — clone it to project or global scope to customize.
editor.staleBanner: This snippet was changed externally — your save was rejected.
editor.savedToast: Saved. Available via %name% in your next chat message.

warnings.selfRef: This snippet references itself (%{{name}}%) — runtime expansion will loop.

favorites.rowAria: Favorite {{command}}, position {{index}} of {{total}}. Use Arrow Up or Arrow Down to reorder.
favorites.empty: No favorites yet — pick frequently used /slash commands to surface them in the chip bar above the chat input.
favorites.noCandidates: No more commands available — every visible /slash command is already starred.
favorites.maxReached: You can star at most {{max}} commands. Remove one to add another.

empty.title: No snippets configured.
empty.description: Create a snippet to expand %name% references in chat input.

error.fileExists: A snippet with this name already exists at the target location.
error.bundledReadOnly: Bundled snippets are read-only. Clone to project or global scope to customize.

gitignorePatternMissing.toastDetail: Saving to `{{sibling}}` would still be tracked by git. Add `{{pattern}}` to `.gitignore` so the local sibling is excluded.
gitignorePatternMissing.fallbackHint: Add `{{pattern}}` to your project `.gitignore` manually, then retry the save.

modeA.title: Team-shared harness project
modeA.detail: Files like `.claude/settings.json` are tracked by git.

modeB.title: Private harness mode
modeB.detail: `.claude/` is excluded from git. To share with the team, use the bundle export.

secretOnShared.title: Plaintext secret detected
secretOnShared.body: The save was blocked because this file is tracked by git and contains what looks like a secret token. Move the value to a `*.local.*` sibling so it is not committed.

action.markNotSecret: Mark this value as not a secret

routing.apiErrorToast: Could not route the save. Check your connection and try again.

secretOnShared.autoCreateSiblingNotice: A new file `{{path}}` will be created.
secretOnShared.errorMessage: Plaintext secret detected. Save was blocked.
secretOnShared.localBadgeLockTooltip: Bearer tokens or other secrets are present — this file is local-only and is not committed to git.

lint.listEmpty: No lint issues in this section.

namingDuplicate.title: Duplicate name across sources
namingDuplicate.description: The same name exists in more than one scope (project / user / plugin) or twice in the same scope.

hookMatcherRegexInvalid.title: Invalid hook matcher regex
hookMatcherRegexInvalid.description: The matcher field is parsed as a JavaScript RegExp; invalid expressions never fire.

parseYamlJsonError.title: Parse error
parseYamlJsonError.description: Frontmatter or JSON could not be parsed; the entry is shadowed until fixed.

mcpCommandNotOnPath.title: MCP command not on PATH
mcpCommandNotOnPath.description: The stdio MCP server's command was not found on the Hammoc server's PATH.
mcpCommandNotOnPath.message: Command "{{command}}" for server {{name}} could not be resolved on the server PATH.
mcpCommandNotOnPath.serverPathNotice: This check uses the Hammoc server process's PATH — your CLI shell (different shell, container, or Windows vs WSL) may resolve it differently. To turn off this rule, open Lint rules in the workbench.

mcpUrlInvalid.title: Invalid MCP URL
mcpUrlInvalid.description: The url field for sse / http / ws transports must be a parseable URL with an allowed protocol (http / https / ws / wss).

agentToolsNonStandard.title: Non-standard tool in agent.tools
agentToolsNonStandard.description: The tool name is not in the standard Claude Code tool list. Whether sub-agents may reference MCP tools (mcp__server__tool) directly is not officially documented; we surface this as a warning only.
agentToolsNonStandard.message: Tool "{{tool}}" on agent {{agent}} is not in the standard list — direct MCP-tool references are unverified, so this surfaces as a warning, never an error.

hookEnvVarUndefined.title: Undefined environment variable in hook
hookEnvVarUndefined.description: The hook command or prompt references ${VAR} that is not defined on the Hammoc server's process.env.
hookEnvVarUndefined.message: Variable {{variable}} referenced by hook "{{hook}}" is not set on the Hammoc server.

preferences.title: Lint rules
preferences.toggleErrorToast: Failed to update lint rule. Please retry.

ruleDescription.namingDuplicate: Warn when the same name resolves to more than one scope or appears twice in the same scope.
ruleDescription.hookMatcherRegexInvalid: Error when a hook matcher cannot be compiled as a JavaScript RegExp.
ruleDescription.parseYamlJsonError: Error when frontmatter / JSON parsing failed in any of the 5 harness domains.
ruleDescription.mcpCommandNotOnPath: Warn when an MCP stdio server's command is not on the Hammoc server's PATH. Off by default — Hammoc server PATH and your CLI PATH may legitimately differ.
ruleDescription.mcpUrlInvalid: Error when a sse / http / ws server's URL cannot be parsed or uses a disallowed protocol.
ruleDescription.agentToolsNonStandard: Warn when a sub-agent lists a tool name outside the standard set. Direct MCP-tool references are surfaced as warnings, never errors.
ruleDescription.hookEnvVarUndefined: Warn when a ${VAR} reference in a hook command is not defined on the Hammoc server's process.env.

secretsPolicy.excludedDetail: All secrets are stripped from the bundle.
secretsPolicy.placeholderDetail: Secrets are replaced with environment-variable references.
secretsPolicy.includedExplicitDetail: Plaintext secrets are included as-is. Risky.
secretsPolicy.includedExplicitConfirm: I understand this bundle will contain plaintext secrets.
secretsPolicy.includedExplicitFilenameNotice: The filename will include WITH-SECRETS to flag the contents.
secretsPolicy.includedExplicitDownloadToast: Plaintext secrets included — share only over a trusted channel.
secretsPolicy.importIncludedSecretsWarning: This bundle contains plaintext secrets. Confirm the source before proceeding.
secretsPolicy.excludedImportToast: The bundle was exported with secrets stripped — affected fields will be empty.
secretsPolicy.placeholderImportToast: The bundle uses placeholder secrets — fill in the ${ENV_REF} values after import.

importPreview.title: Import preview
importPreview.applyPartialRollback: {{count}} items applied, then rolled back due to a failure.
importPreview.applyAbort: Apply aborted. No files were changed.

pluginDependencies.bannerDetail: This bundle depends on {{total}} plugin(s); {{missing}} not installed.
pluginDependencies.installFallbackToast: Install {{name}} from the {{marketplace}} marketplace, then retry.

compat.futureBundleTitle: Bundle from a newer Hammoc version
compat.futureBundleDetail: This bundle was created on a newer Hammoc release (bundleVersion={{bundleVersion}}). The current version (1) cannot import it safely.
compat.unknownSectionWarning: Section "{{name}}" is unknown to this Hammoc version and will be skipped.
compat.malformedManifestDetail: The manifest file is missing or could not be parsed. See details below.

marketplace.emptyHint: Add a marketplace to browse installable plugins.
marketplace.noMatches: No plugins match the current filters.

modal.cliHint: Paste this command into your Claude CLI session.
modal.cliOnlyNote: Plugin commands run in an external Claude CLI session — they are not executed from the Hammoc chat.

formatWarning.title: Plugin install file format not recognized

reason.unrecognizedVersion: An unrecognized installed_plugins.json version was detected; installed state may be incomplete.
reason.unexpectedShape: installed_plugins.json has an unexpected shape; installed state may be incomplete.
reason.parseError: installed_plugins.json could not be parsed; installed state is unavailable.

permissionMode.planDescription: Claude only plans and does not modify files
permissionMode.askDescription: Asks for approval before modifying files
permissionMode.classifierDescription: Auto-approves safe tool calls; only risky ones ask for confirmation
permissionMode.selectorAria: Permission mode: {{label}}. {{description}}. Click to switch to next mode

webPush.title: Web Push Notifications
webPush.description: Receive browser push notifications on this device. Works on Android, desktop browsers, and iOS PWA (16.4+).
webPush.iosHint: On iOS, Web Push only works when Hammoc is installed as a PWA (Add to Home Screen).
webPush.notSupported: Web Push is not available. A secure connection (HTTPS) is required and your browser must support Web Push.
webPush.permissionDenied: Notification permission was denied. Please enable it in your browser settings.
webPush.enableHint: When enabled, push notifications are sent alongside Telegram (if configured).
webPush.subscribeSuccess: Push notifications enabled for this browser.
webPush.subscribeError: Failed to subscribe. Please try again.
webPush.unsubscribeSuccess: Push notifications disabled for this browser.
webPush.unsubscribeError: Failed to unsubscribe. Please try again.
webPush.loadError: Failed to load Web Push settings.
